domain: Chumash Tomol plank canoe (California Channel Islands maritime culture; plank-built boat with keel/floor + 3-4 rows of stacked planks; tok dogbane-fiber sewing + yop pine-pitch+asphalt sealing between plank rows; frameless multi-layer hull with flexibility + waterproofing emerging from stacked construction; described as most technologically complex watercraft in pre-contact North America)
specific_mechanism: TOMOL-STACK: layered modular memory architecture with cross-tier sealant + seam embeddings. (1) Base keel = parametric long-term memory. (2) Stacked plank rows = 3-4 modular memory tiers (T_1 short-term FIFO buffer, T_2 episodic memory, T_3 semantic graph, T_4 procedural skill cache). (3) Cross-tier yop-sealant: small learned projection P_seal between adjacent tiers preventing memory leakage while allowing structured cross-tier access. (4) Tok-seam embeddings: per-tier interface vectors connecting adjacent layers via cross-tier attention (analog of fiber stitching). (5) Frameless flexible access: no rigid orchestrator — memory access routes tier-walking through seam embeddings.
llm_application: TOMOL-STACK for LLM agent memory: 3-4 stacked modular memory tiers + cross-tier yop-sealant projection + tok-seam embeddings + tier-walking access. Differs from Multi-Layered Memory 2603.29194 (multi-layer memory for LLM agents — closest, but no cross-tier yop-sealant + seam embeddings). MemOS L1-L2-L3 (L1 traces + L2 policies + L3 world models + skills — no cross-tier sealant + seam embedding). Memori 2603.19935 (persistent memory layer — single layer, not stacked). Serokell Design Patterns (primary + external memory + paging — no cross-tier sealant + seam embedding). Differs from R408 ICELANDIC-CHAIN (daisy-chain serial, not stacked), R428 WAVE-BOWL-CACHE (concentric-ring priority, not stacked tiers), R442 ADINKRA-SYMBOL-LM (canonical-symbol catalog), R454 CHILOTE-PALAFITO (3-tier HBM-DRAM-SSD with flexible-pile base) by EXPLICIT CROSS-TIER YOP-SEALANT PROJECTION + TOK-SEAM EMBEDDING + TIER-WALKING ACCESS.
novelty_claim: Multi-Layered Memory + MemOS + Memori + Serokell Design Patterns cover modular tier memory paradigm. TOMOL-STACK adds (a) cross-tier yop-sealant learned projection + (b) tok-seam embedding cross-tier attention + (c) frameless tier-walking access. Recombination — cross-tier attention is mainstream.
